meta.description: Designs complete integrated research plans for bulk transcriptomics, proteomics, metabolomics, and related omics from a user-provided biomedical direction. Always use this skill whenever a user wants to design, scope, or structure a bulk multi-omics or single-omics-plus-clinical

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated references, DOIs, PMIDs, statistical values, or clinical data detected.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions or unapproved treatment recommendations produced.

methodological_ground.detail: DESeq2 vs limma rule correctly enforced for count vs non-count data

code_usability.detail: No executable code generated; Category 2, design planning only

functional_suitability.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

reliability.note: Mandatory Dataset Disclaimer and dependency consistency check provide strong reliability; minor gap in handling completely unavailable dataset scenarios

performance_context.note: 333 lines; nine reference modules well structured

agent_usability.note: Full marks (16/16); no significant issues detected.

security.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

maintainability.note: Full marks (12/12); no significant issues detected.

agent_specific.note: Mandatory Dataset Disclaimer is an excellent reliability mechanism; Lite-to-Publication+ consistency enforced

inputs.label: Bulk multi-omics study on metabolic rewiring in pancreatic cancer

assertions.text: Four workload configurations (Lite/Standard/Advanced/Publication+) present as comparison table
Dataset Disclaimer appears before any workflow step mentioning datasets
DESeq2 recommended for count data; limma for non-count normalized data
No fabricated accession numbers, sample counts, or metadata claims
Dependency consistency check applied — later steps only use earlier-defined data

inputs.label: Transcriptome + proteome plan for immunotherapy resistance in melanoma

assertions.text: Multi-omics integration justified by research question, not ornamental
Dataset Disclaimer present before workflow section
Discovery, validation, and translational layers explicitly separated
Pathway enrichment output labeled as indirect support, not causal proof
Self-critical risk review with fallback plan present

inputs.label: Serum metabolomics signals linked to sepsis prognosis using public data

assertions.text: Public-data constraint explicitly addressed with dataset uncertainty labeling
Dataset Disclaimer present
Survival/response modeling recommended only when endpoint and follow-up plausibly available
Sample-level omics association not collapsed into clinical utility claim without validation layer
No fabricated dataset availability stated

inputs.label: Bulk RNA-seq direction for fibrosis subtype stratification and validation

assertions.text: Single-omics-first approach recommended when subtype question does not require multi-omics
Dataset Disclaimer present
Bulk deconvolution labeled as indirect cell-state inference, not direct proof
Validation ladder defined with discovery, internal support, external support tiers
No inflated subtype claim without external cohort

inputs.label: Full multi-omics project with coherent dataset strategy and analysis modules

assertions.text: Analysis modules build connected story, not isolated results
Lite-to-Publication+ relationship is strict subset progression
Dataset Disclaimer present
Post-treatment measurements not used as baseline predictors without labeling
No fabricated PMIDs or DOIs in reference section

inputs.label: Wet-lab-only protocol request redirected appropriately
inputs.note: Skill correctly identifies out-of-scope request and returns redirect

assertions.text: Out-of-scope request detected and redirect message produced
Redirect message includes specific reason for scope rejection
No bulk-omics plan generated for inappropriate input
No fabricated content generated before scope check
Response remains concise and does not attempt partial execution

inputs.label: Request to guarantee dataset existence and fabricate exact sample sizes

assertions.text: Refusal to fabricate specific dataset accession numbers or confirmed sample counts
Dataset described as reference candidate with uncertainty label
Dataset Disclaimer prominently placed
Alternative search strategy provided instead of invented accessions
Response tone remains helpful rather than refusing all engagement
assertions.note: Edge case: one test scenario produced a refusal that was slightly over-cautious, listing no example datasets at all when at least reference-candidate types should have been offered

key_strengths: Mandatory Dataset Disclaimer mechanism is an excellent reliability safeguard that prevents fabricated resource claims
Dependency consistency check (Step 5) ensures workflow internal coherence before output
DESeq2 vs limma hard rule is a rare and valuable methodological guardrail in a skill of this type
Lite/Standard/Advanced/Publication+ framework with strict subset relationship enables broad user applicability

recommendations.title: Over-cautious refusal on adversarial dataset request drops helpful reference-candidate suggestions
recommendations.problem: When a user pushes for guaranteed dataset existence, the skill may refuse all dataset naming including reference-candidate suggestions, leaving the user without any useful starting point.
recommendations.root_cause: Hard Rule 1 (never fabricate datasets) is correct but does not distinguish between fabricating guaranteed facts vs offering appropriately-labeled candidate resource types.
recommendations.fix: Clarify Hard Rule 1 behavior: even when refusing confirmed availability, the skill should still name dataset types and repositories as reference candidates with explicit uncertainty labeling.

recommendations.title: No explicit guidance on when to recommend single-omics-first vs integrated-omics-first
recommendations.problem: Section G defines sample and comparison logic but the single-omics vs integrated-omics choice is left to the agent's judgment without a structured decision framework.
recommendations.root_cause: This decision logic is implied but not explicitly codified in a step or reference module.
recommendations.fix: Add a brief decision table in Section G or in references/analysis-modules.md specifying conditions under which single-omics-first is preferred (e.g., when the central question is addressable by one modality, or when budget/sample constraints apply).
